results.title: Fetch API - MDN Web Docs
results.description: The Fetch API provides an interface for fetching resources (including across the network). It is a more powerful and flexible replacement for XMLHttpRequest.

results.title: Using the Fetch API - MDN Web Docs
results.description: The Fetch API provides a JavaScript interface for making HTTP requests and processing the responses. Fetch is the modern replacement for XMLHttpRequest.

results.title: Request - Web APIs - MDN Web Docs
results.description: The Request interface of the Fetch API represents a resource request. You can create a new Request object using the Request() constructor.

results.title: Using Deferred Fetch - Web APIs | MDN

results.title: Response - Web APIs - MDN Web Docs - Mozilla
results.description: The Response interface of the Fetch API represents the response to a request. You can create a new Response object using the Response() constructor.

results.title: Window: fetch() method - Web APIs | MDN

results.title: Web APIs - MDN Web Docs - Mozilla
results.description: Below is a list of all the APIs and interfaces (object types) that you may be able to use while developing your Web app or site.

results.title: ServiceWorkerGlobalScope: fetch event - Web APIs | MDN
results.description: The fetch event of the ServiceWorkerGlobalScope interface is fired in the service worker's global scope when the main app thread makes a network request.
